diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden unresponsive collapse is the first limb of arrest recognition.
Syncope also collapses suddenly, but rouses quickly — sustained unresponsiveness argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Agonal (occasional gasping) respiration — not breathing normally
symptoms.character: irregular gasps every few seconds, no regular breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In an unresponsive person, agonal gasping is a sign of cardiac arrest and mandates immediate CPR; it is not effective breathing.
A fainted patient breathes normally; agonal gasping excludes simple syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe chest pain immediately preceding collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ischaemic chest pain preceding a sudden arrest points to ACS as the precipitant of a shockable rhythm.
Establishes a cardiac (likely ischaemic) mechanism for the arrest.
Visible central cyanosis reflects absent effective circulation/oxygenation, consistent with arrest.
Flaccidity accompanies loss of cerebral perfusion in arrest.
Active seizure produces tonic-clonic movement, not flaccidity — points away from seizure.
Absent central pulse confirms circulatory arrest — a trained clinician sign, sealed; lay rescuers are NOT asked to check a pulse before starting CPR.
Reflects cerebral hypoperfusion; a clinician examination finding, sealed and not elicitable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unrecordable blood pressure and absent heart rate on monitoring

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective absence of circulation — sealed clinician/monitor measurement, unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Shockable rhythm — ventricular fibrillation (or pulseless VT)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without applying an AED, a shockable rhythm cannot be identified or treated — defibrillation is the definitive reversible step.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: AED analysis determines shockable vs non-shockable; a sealed investigation result, obtained by attaching the device — the telehealth AI coaches the bystander to apply it and follow its prompts.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Unresponsive + not breathing normally is the complete lay-rescuer trigger to start CPR and get a defibrillator — no further signs are needed before acting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Apply an AED and follow its voice prompts
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Early defibrillation of a shockable rhythm is the single highest-yield intervention; the AI coaches the bystander to fetch and attach the nearest AED without interrupting compressions.
After return of spontaneous circulation, identifies the ischaemic precipitant and guides reperfusion — a hospital investigation, never available in telehealth in real time.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Lay rescuer manual pulse check before CPR
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Bystanders should NOT delay compressions to search for a pulse — pulse checks are unreliable for lay rescuers and waste critical seconds.
